hints.invenietis and aperietur: 'Invenietis' is future indicative and 'aperietur' is future passive — not imperatives
hints.Quaerite and invenietis: 'Invenietis' is future indicative, not imperative — 'Quaerite' and 'pulsate' are imperative
hints.pulsate and aperietur: 'Aperietur' is future passive, not imperative — 'Quaerite' and 'pulsate' are imperative

hints.to ask: That would be 'rogare/petere' — 'Quaerite' comes from 'quaerere' (to seek)
hints.to complain: That would be 'queri' — 'Quaerite' comes from 'quaerere' (to seek)
hints.to rest: That would be 'quiescere' — 'Quaerite' comes from 'quaerere' (to seek)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd pl. would be 'quaeritis' — 'Quaerite' is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'quaeratis' — 'Quaerite' is imperative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'quaeretis' — 'Quaerite' is present imperative

hints.queror, queri, questus sum, —: These are the parts of 'queri' (to complain) — a deponent verb
hints.quiesco, quiescere, quievi, quietum: These are the parts of 'quiescere' (to rest)
hints.rogo, rogare, rogavi, rogatum: These are the parts of 'rogare' (to ask)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'invenitis' — 'invenietis' with -ie- is future (4th conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'inveniatis' — 'invenietis' is future indicative

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come) — 'invenire' is in + venire
hints.reperio, reperire, repperi, repertum: These are the parts of 'reperire' (to find) — a synonym
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)

hints.to push: That would be 'pellere/trudere' — 'pulsate' comes from 'pulsare' (to knock/beat)
hints.to pulse: English 'pulse' derives from Latin, but 'pulsare' primarily means 'to knock/beat'
hints.to strike: While related, 'pulsare' in this context means 'to knock (at a door)'

hints.pello, pellere, pepuli, pulsum: These are the parts of 'pellere' (to drive/push) — 'pulsare' is its frequentative
hints.pungo, pungere, pupugi, punctum: These are the parts of 'pungere' (to prick/sting)
hints.premo, premere, pressi, pressum: These are the parts of 'premere' (to press)

hints.present indicative passive: Present passive would be 'aperitur' — 'aperietur' with -ie- is future passive
hints.present subjunctive passive: Subjunctive passive would be 'aperiatur' — 'aperietur' is future indicative
hints.perfect indicative passive: Perfect passive would be 'apertum est' — 'aperietur' is future

hints.operio, operire, operui, opertum: These are the parts of 'operire' (to cover) — the opposite of 'aperire'
hints.pario, parere, peperi, partum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to give birth)
hints.reperio, reperire, repperi, repertum: These are the parts of 'reperire' (to find)

hints.ablative: While 'vobis' could be ablative, with 'aperietur' it is dative (for whom it opens)

translation: Seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you
